title: HelpAI
description: Machine-readable help emitted by a Portunix plugin when invoked with --help-ai. Describes the tool identity and its commands (parameters, examples) so AI harnesses and consumers such as the portunix-vscode extension can discover plugin capabilities without parsing human-readable help text. Plugin-agnostic and additive: new optional fields (e.g. per-command supported_extensions / supported_mime_types) may be added without breaking older plugin outputs.

tool.description: Tool identifier as invoked on the CLI (e.g. 'text-extractor', 'ptx-redmine').

version.description: Plugin version string (e.g. '1.1.10', '1.1.10-SNAPSHOT'). Free-form to allow pre-release/build suffixes.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the plugin does.

name.description: Command name as typed on the CLI, possibly multi-word (e.g. 'convert', 'extract text').

brief.description: Short one-line summary of the command.

description.description: Full human-readable description of the command.

name.description: Parameter name. May be a positional name (e.g. 'file') or a flag (e.g. '--last-hours').

type.description: Parameter value type. Free-form to accommodate per-plugin conventions (e.g. 'string', 'boolean', 'integer', 'int', 'bool').

supported_extensions.description: Optional (added v1.0.0, #091). Input file extensions this command accepts, lowercase with a leading dot (e.g. '.pdf', '.docx'). Plugin-agnostic: any command that consumes files may declare it so consumers can filter inputs without parsing description text.

supported_mime_types.description: Optional (added v1.0.0, #091). MIME types this command accepts (e.g. 'application/pdf', 'audio/mpeg'). Complements supported_extensions where a MIME-based match is more precise.

global_flags.description: Optional. Flags accepted by every command of the plugin (e.g. '--verbose', '--config'). Emitted by plugins such as ptx-redmine (#060).
